zh.lifeSummary: 當William Fowke於1403年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布霍德時，他的父親Richard Fowke23歲，母親Anne Allen24歲。他於大約1424年與Ann Eyton在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布霍德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Fowke于1403年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布霍德时，他的父亲Richard Fowke23岁，母亲Anne Allen24岁。他于大约1424年与Ann Eyton在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布霍德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Fowke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from the Middle English, Anglo-Norman French personal name Folk, Fulk, Fuke. Compare Foxon . For a variant form of the personal name see Foulkes . See also Fudge , which may sometimes be a variant form.

variants.name: Fudge
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Anglo-Norman French personal name Fulch or Fuche, usually a pet form of Old French Fulcher, Fucher (see Fulcher ). Voicing of ‘ch’ to soft ‘g’ gave rise to Fudge. The name may sometimes have been interchangeable with Fowke (for which see Foulk 1), derived from a pet form of this and other Old French personal names in Fulc-.
English: habitational name from Fuge, in Blackawton, Devon. This placename is composed of Old English feoh ‘cattle’ + wīc ‘farm, dairy farm, village’.

variants.name: Foulk
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English and Anglo-Norman French personal name Fulk, which is of ancient Germanic origin, ultimately from an element meaning ‘folk, people’.

variants.name: Fogg
meanings: English (northern): possibly a variant of Fowke (see Foulk 1). It may derive from Middle English fogge ‘aftermath’ (i.e. grass left to grow after the hay has been cut, also applied to long grass in a water meadow), especially in northern England, where the term occurs as a specifying element in minor placenames, but as it is not found on its own as a simplex name, the derivation is doubtful without some supporting evidence. The word fogge, which is probably of Old Norse origin, is still in use as a dialect term in Craven, Yorkshire, and in eastern Lancashire. modern English fog ‘thick mist’ is first attested in the 16th century and is unlikely to be the source of the surname.

variants.name: Fuke
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Fowke   and  compare  Fook  .

variants.name: Folk
meanings: German: variant of   Volk   1 and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in Czechia.

variants.name: Focke
meanings: North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Folco, a pet form of various names beginning with Folc- or Volk-; cognate with Foulk 1.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Foulk   1. See also   Foulks  .

variants.name: Foulke
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Shropshire): variant of   Foulk  .

variants.name: Fulk
meanings: Altered form of German   Volk   or   Falk  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Fowke was born in 1403, in Brewood, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Fowke, was 23 and his mother, Anne Allen, was 24. He married Ann Eyton about 1424, in Brewood, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Fowke est né en 1403, à Brewood, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Fowke, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Anne Allen, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Ann Eyton environ 1424, à Brewood, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Fowke 1403 in Brewood, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Fowke 23 und seine Mutter Anne Allen 24. Er hat um 1424 in Brewood, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Ann Eyton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Fowke è nato il nel 1403 nel Brewood, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard Fowke, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Anne Allen, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Ann Eyton circa 1424, nel Brewood, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: William Fowkeが1403年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブレウッドで生まれた時，父Richard Fowkeは23歳，母Anne Allenは24歳であった。約1424年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブレウッドでAnn Eytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Fowke(이)가 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 브레우드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Fowke(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Allen(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1424년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 브레우드에서 Ann Eyton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Fowke nasceu em 1403, em Brewood, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Fowke, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Anne Allen, tinha 24 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Fowke родился в 1403 г. в местности брюуд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Fowke было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Allen, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Eyton около 1424 г. в местности брюуд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в его родном городе.
